zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Mary Elizabeth Lewis Carter于1506年出生在英国格盧斯特斯爾时，她的父亲Thomas ap John Lewis Lord of Turberville and Coytie22岁，母亲Margery verch John17岁。她于1526年与Sir James Evarts Everard结婚。她去世于1588年8月30日在英国英格兰素瑞享年82岁。

nameMeanings.name: Carter
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: occupational name for a transporter of goods, from Middle English carter(e) ‘carter’ (Anglo-Norman French car(e)tier, Old French charetier, medieval Latin carettarius, carettator). The Old French word coalesced with the earlier Middle English word cart(e) ‘cart’, which is from either Old Norse kartr or Old English cræt, both of which, like the Late Latin word, were probably derived from Celtic. This surname is also very common among African Americans.
Irish: shortened form of   McCarter  .
Americanized form of German   Karter   ‘carder’.

variants.name: Charman
meanings: English (Sussex and Surrey): occupational name for a carter, carrier, or coachman, from Middle English (Old French) char, car ‘car, cart, carriage’ + Middle English man ‘man’. Compare Carter and Carman 2.

English (Sussex and Surrey): occupational name for someone who did odd jobs, from Middle English char(re), chere (Old English cier(r), cer(r), cear(r), cær(r)) ‘turn (of activity), small job, chore’ + man. Compare Carman 2.
In some cases probably also an Americanized form of Slovenian Čarman (see Carman 5).

variants.name: Chartier
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Andre, Armand, Normand, Jacques, Marcel, Adelard, Alcide, Emile, Gilles, Raoul, Rosaire, Alphonse.
French: shortened form of charretier, an occupational name for a carter, from an agent derivative of Old French charette ‘cart’.
French Canadian: altered form of English   Carter  , a cognate of 1 above. Compare   Sharkey   and   Shorkey  .

variants.name: Karter
meanings: German: occupational name for a carder (someone who prepares wool for spinning), from Middle High German karter. Compare Carter .

variants.name: McCarter
meanings: Scottish and northern Irish: variant of   McArthur  .

variants.name: Carrier
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Armand, Andre, Marcel, Emile, Lucien, Cecile, Alain, Gaetan, Gilles, Jacques, Laurent, Normand.

English and southern French: from Middle English, Old French car(r)ier (from Late Latin carrarius, a derivative of carrum ‘cart, wagon’, a word of Gaulish origin), an occupational name for a carter (see Carter ), in French also for a cartwright. Compare French Charrier .
French: occupational name for a stonemason or quarryman, carrier. Compare Coyer .

variants.name: Cartman
meanings: English (Yorkshire and Lancashire): occupational name from Middle English cartman ‘man who operated a cart for the transport of goods’. Compare Carter .
Americanized form of German Kartmann (see Kartman ).

nameMeanings.name: Mary
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From Latin Maria via Old Welsh Meir. See Mary .

nameMeanings.name: Elizabeth
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: The usual spelling of   Elisabeth   in English. It is recorded in the medieval period, but was made popular by being borne by  Queen Elizabeth I  of England (  1533–1603 ). In the 20th century it again became extremely fashionable, partly because it was the name of  Elizabeth Bowes-Lyon  (  1900–2002 ), who in  1936  became Queen Elizabeth as the wife of King George VI, and after his death in  1952  achieved great public affection as Queen Mother for nearly half a century. Even more influentially, it is the name of her daughter  Queen Elizabeth II  (  b. 1926 ).

variants.name: Elisabeth
meanings: The spelling of Elizabeth used in the Authorized Version of the New Testament, and in most modern European languages. This was the name of the mother of John the Baptist (Luke 1:60). Etymologically, the name means ‘God is my oath’, and is therefore identical with Elisheba, the name of the wife of Aaron according to the genealogy at Exodus 6:23. The final element seems to have been altered by association with Hebrew shabbāth ‘Sabbath’.

nameMeanings.name: Lewis
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: form, since the Middle Ages, of the French name Louis . In modern use it is also in part a transferred use of the surname derived from this given name, or from Anglicized forms of Gaelic Mac Lughaidh ‘son of Lughaidh ’ or Welsh Llewelyn .

variants.name: Lewys
meanings: form, since the Middle Ages, of the French name Louis . In modern use it is also in part a transferred use of the surname derived from this given name, or from Anglicized forms of Gaelic Mac Lughaidh ‘son of Lughaidh ’ or Welsh Llewelyn .

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

historicEvents.title: The Royal Exchange is Organized
historicEvents.description: The Royal Exchange was organized in January 1571 by Sir Thomas Gresham. Gresham is known as the father of English banking.

historicEvents.title: The First English Colony is Established in America
historicEvents.description: England established its first colony in North America in 1585 as a way to generate additional wealth. The colony was named Virginia, after the virgin Queen Elizabeth I.

en.lifeSummary: When Mary Elizabeth Lewis Carter was born in 1506, in Gloucestershire, England, her father, Thomas ap John Lewis Lord of Turberville and Coytie, was 22 and her mother, Margery verch John, was 17. She married Sir James Evarts Everard in 1526. She died on 30 August 1588, in Surrey, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 82.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Mary Elizabeth Lewis Carter est née en 1506, à Gloucestershire, Angleterre, son père, Thomas ap John Lewis Lord of Turberville and Coytie, avait 22 ans et sa mère, Margery verch John, avait 17 ans. Elle a épousé Sir James Evarts Everard en 1526. Elle est décédée le 30 août 1588, à Surrey, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 82 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Mary Elizabeth Lewis Carter 1506 in Gloucestershire, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Thomas ap John Lewis Lord of Turberville and Coytie 22 und ihre Mutter Margery verch John 17. Sie hat 1526 Sir James Evarts Everard geheiratet. Sie starb am 30. August 1588 im Alter von 82 Jahren in Surrey, England, United Kingdom.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Mary Elizabeth Lewis Carter è nata il nel 1506 nel Gloucestershire, England, sua padre, Thomas ap John Lewis Lord of Turberville and Coytie, aveva 22 anni e sua madre, Margery verch John, aveva 17 anni. Lei sposato Sir James Evarts Everard nel 1526. Morì il 30 agosto 1588, nel Surrey, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, all'età di 82 anni.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Mary Elizabeth Lewis Carter родилась в 1506 г. в местности глоусестиршир, Англия, её отцу по имени Thomas ap John Lewis Lord of Turberville and Coytie было 22 года, а матери, которую звали Margery verch John, 17.
